name: English Full Breakfast Plate
steps: Fry the bacon until crisp, then set aside.
Fry the eggs in half the butter.
Warm the tomatoes in a small pan until heated through.
Fry the bread in the remaining butter until golden, and plate everything together.
steps_detailed: Fry the bacon over medium heat until crisp, 3-4 minutes per side, then set aside, keeping a little of the rendered fat in the pan if you like.
Fry the eggs in half the butter over medium heat, seasoning with black pepper.
Warm the tomatoes in a small pan, 4-5 minutes, until heated through and slightly reduced.
Fry the bread in the remaining butter until deeply golden on both sides, and plate everything together.
Bacon, egg, and fried bread are the three components that appear in essentially every version of a full English, however many extras a given kitchen adds around them.

name: English Porridge with Currants
steps: Simmer the oats in the milk for 5-6 minutes, stirring, until thick and creamy.
Stir in the currants and top with the honey.
steps_detailed: Simmer the oats in the milk with the cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring often, 6-8 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Stir in most of the currants, reserving a few to scatter on top, and finish with the honey.

name: English Kedgeree
steps: Cook the rice until tender.
Flake the kippered herring and warm it through with the turmeric.
Fold the fish into the rice and top with the sliced egg.
steps_detailed: Cook the rice until tender.
Flake the kippered herring and warm it through in the butter with the turmeric, 3-4 minutes.
Fold the fish gently into the rice, top with the sliced egg and cilantro, and serve warm.
Kedgeree began as an Anglo-Indian breakfast brought home by colonial-era travelers and has stayed a fixture of English breakfast tables ever since.

name: English Cottage Pie
steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Add the tomatoes and simmer 10 minutes.
Spread the mashed potato over the top and bake at 400F for 15-20 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and simmer 12-15 minutes until thickened.
Mash the potato with the butter and milk until smooth, spread over the beef, and bake at 400F (200C) for 18-22 minutes until the top is golden.
Cottage pie is made with beef specifically - the lamb version goes by shepherd's pie, a distinction English cooks tend to hold to firmly.

name: English Cheddar and Leek Bake
steps: Saute the leeks in the butter until soft, about 8 minutes.
Stir in the flour, then the cheese, and cook until melted and thickened.
Bake at 375F for 10 minutes until bubbling.
steps_detailed: Saute the leeks in the butter over medium-low heat until fully soft and sweet, 10-12 minutes.
Stir in the flour and cook 1 minute, then add the milk gradually, followed by most of the cheese, stirring until melted and thickened.
Transfer to a small baking dish, top with the remaining cheese, and bake at 375F (190C) for 10-12 minutes until bubbling and lightly browned.

name: English Baked Fish and Chips
steps: Toss the potato wedges with the oil and roast at 425F for 20-25 minutes, turning once.
Bake the fish alongside for the last 12-15 minutes until just cooked through.
Serve with a squeeze of lime.
steps_detailed: Toss the potato wedges with the oil and roast at 425F (220C) for 22-28 minutes, turning once, until crisp and golden.
Dust the fish lightly with the flour and pepper and bake alongside for the last 12-15 minutes until just cooked through and flaking.
Serve with a squeeze of lime standing in for the malt vinegar traditionally sprinkled over a proper chip-shop portion.

name: English Ploughman's Lunch

ingredients.note: standing in for a crusty bread roll

steps: Arrange the cheese, bread, and a small dab of mustard together.
Toss the shredded cabbage with the honey and serve alongside.

ingredients_full.note: standing in for a crusty bread roll

steps_detailed: Arrange the cheese, bread, and a small dab of mustard together on a board.
Toss the shredded cabbage with the honey and lime juice and serve alongside as a simple, bright slaw standing in for pickled onion or chutney.
A ploughman's is less a recipe than an assembly - cold cheese, bread, mustard, and something pickled or sweet, built to be eaten with the hands over a pub lunch.

name: English Sunday Roast Beef with Potatoes and Cabbage

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Roast the beef at 375F for 35-40 minutes until tender.
Roast the potatoes alongside in the butter until golden.
Steam the cabbage in the last 8-10 minutes and serve everything together.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Season the beef with the pepper and garlic and roast at 375F (190C) for 40-45 minutes until tender.
Toss the potatoes in half the butter and roast alongside, turning once, until deeply golden.
Steam the cabbage in the last 10 minutes, toss with the remaining butter, and serve everything together.
The Sunday roast, built around a large joint of meat and whatever vegetables are in season, remains the week's centerpiece meal in many English households.

name: English Shepherd's Pie
steps: Brown the lamb with the onion.
Add the tomatoes and simmer 10 minutes.
Spread the mashed potato over the top and bake at 400F for 15-20 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Brown the lamb with the onion and pepper over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and simmer 12-15 minutes until thickened.
Mash the potato with the butter until smooth, spread over the lamb, and bake at 400F (200C) for 18-22 minutes until golden.
Shepherd's pie specifically means lamb - the name itself points back to shepherds rather than cattle drovers.

name: English Bangers and Mash
steps: Form the beef into sausage shapes and pan-fry until browned through.
Mash the potato with half the butter.
Soften the onion in the remaining butter for the gravy and serve alongside.
steps_detailed: Form the beef into sausage shapes and pan-fry over medium heat, 10-12 minutes, turning, until well browned through.
Mash the potato with half the butter and the milk until smooth.

Soften the onion in the remaining butter, 8-10 minutes, stir in the flour, and thin with a splash of water into a loose gravy - bangers and mash, built around whatever gravy is on hand, is a weeknight staple across England.

name: English Fish Pie
steps: Poach the fish gently in the milk until just cooked, then flake it.
Spread the flaked fish in a small dish, top with the mashed potato and cheese, and bake at 375F for 15-18 minutes.
steps_detailed: Poach the fish gently in the milk over low heat, 6-8 minutes, until just cooked through, then flake it, reserving the milk.
Mash the potato with the butter and a splash of the reserved milk.
Spread the flaked fish in a small baking dish, season with pepper, top with the mashed potato and cheese, and bake at 375F (190C) for 18-22 minutes until golden and bubbling.

name: English Cheese Scones
steps: Rub the butter into the flour and baking powder until crumbly.
Stir in the cheese and enough milk to form a soft dough, pat into rounds, and bake at 400F for 12-15 minutes.
steps_detailed: Rub the butter into the flour and baking powder until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in most of the cheese and enough milk to form a soft, slightly sticky dough.
Pat into rounds about an inch thick, top with the remaining cheese, and bake at 400F (200C) for 13-16 minutes until risen and golden.

name: English Herring on Toast
steps: Warm the kippered herring gently.
Toast the bread in the butter and top with the fish.
steps_detailed: Warm the kippered herring gently in a dry pan, 2-3 minutes.
Toast the bread in the butter until golden, season with pepper, and top with the warm fish - kippers on toast are a familiar teatime or light-supper standby.

name: English Currant Scones
steps: Rub the butter into the flour, baking powder, and sugar until crumbly.
Stir in the currants and enough milk to form a soft dough, pat into rounds, and bake at 400F for 12-15 minutes.
steps_detailed: Rub the butter into the flour, baking powder, and sugar until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in the currants and enough milk to form a soft, slightly sticky dough.
Pat into rounds about an inch thick and bake at 400F (200C) for 13-16 minutes until risen and golden - served split with butter, these are a fixture of the English afternoon tea table.

name: English Bubble and Squeak
steps: Fry the cabbage and onion in half the butter until soft.
Mix in the mashed potato and press into a cake in the remaining butter, frying until crisp and golden on both sides.
steps_detailed: Fry the cabbage and onion in half the butter over medium heat, 8-10 minutes, until soft and lightly browned.
Mix in the mashed potato and pepper, press the whole mixture into a cake in the remaining butter, and fry 4-5 minutes per side until crisp and deeply golden.
Bubble and squeak takes its name from the sound it makes in the pan, and was traditionally built from whatever was left over from Sunday's roast.

name: English Sticky Date Pudding

ingredients.note: tbsp, standing in for golden syrup

steps: Simmer the dates in a little water until soft, then mash into the flour and half the butter to form a batter.
Bake at 350F for 20-22 minutes.
Warm the remaining butter with the honey into a sauce and pour over.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, standing in for golden syrup

steps_detailed: Simmer the dates in a little water until very soft, then mash and mix with the flour, baking powder, egg, and half the butter to form a thick batter.
Pour into a small baking dish and bake at 350F (175C) for 22-26 minutes until set and a skewer comes out clean.
Warm the remaining butter with the honey into a glossy sauce and pour generously over the warm pudding.

name: English Bread and Butter Pudding

ingredients.note: torn, standing in for sliced bread

steps: Butter the torn bread and layer it with the currants in a small dish.
Pour the milk over, let it soak 10 minutes, and bake at 350F for 20-25 minutes until set.

ingredients_full.note: torn, standing in for sliced bread

steps_detailed: Butter the torn bread generously and layer it with the currants in a small baking dish.
Whisk the milk, egg, and sugar together and pour over, letting it soak 12-15 minutes.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 22-28 minutes until set and golden on top.

name: English Rice Pudding
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the sugar and cinnamon for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.
Serve warm.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the milk with the sugar, cinnamon, and butter over low heat, stirring often, 28-32 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Serve warm, with a final dusting of cinnamon - a simple, gently spiced pudding found on English tables for generations.

name: English Onion Gravy
steps: Cook the onion slowly in the butter until deeply softened and golden, about 12-15 minutes.
Stir in the flour, then thin with a splash of water into a smooth gravy.
steps_detailed: Cook the onion slowly in the butter over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, 15-18 minutes, until deeply softened and golden brown.
Stir in the flour and pepper, cook 1 minute, then thin gradually with water, stirring, into a smooth, glossy gravy - the standard accompaniment to bangers and mash or a Sunday roast.

name: English Bread Sauce
steps: Simmer the onion in the milk for 8-10 minutes.
Stir in the torn bread and butter, and simmer until thickened into a soft, smooth sauce.
steps_detailed: Simmer the onion in the milk over low heat, 10-12 minutes, until very soft.

Stir in the torn bread, butter, and pepper, and simmer 5-6 more minutes, stirring, until it thickens into a soft, smooth, spoonable sauce - a traditional accompaniment to roast poultry, unusual to outsiders and much loved at home.

name: English Mint Sauce

ingredients.note: tbsp, standing in for the traditional vinegar

steps: Finely chop the mint and combine with the sugar and lime juice.
Let sit 10 minutes so the mint releases its flavor before serving.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, standing in for the traditional vinegar

steps_detailed: Finely chop the mint and combine with the sugar and lime juice, mashing lightly to help release the mint's oils.
Let sit 15-20 minutes so the flavors meld before serving.
Mint sauce, sharp and simple, is the traditional accompaniment to roast lamb across England, cutting through the richness of the meat.

name: English Apple Crumble

ingredients.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Toss the apple with half the sugar and spread in a small baking dish.
Rub the butter into the flour and remaining sugar until crumbly, and scatter over the apple.
Bake at 375F for 25-30 minutes until golden and the apple is tender.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Toss the apple with half the sugar and the cinnamon, and spread in a small baking dish.
Rub the cold butter into the flour and remaining sugar until it resembles coarse crumbs, and scatter evenly over the apple.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 28-32 minutes until the topping is deeply golden and the apple is tender when pierced.
Crumble is one of the simplest and most enduring English puddings - closer to a rustic home bake than a formal dessert, and just as often made with whatever fruit is in season.
